result.stdout:
Description:
  A CLI for ns.tcp.wtf.

Usage:
  TcpWtf.NumberSequence.Tool [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  account                            Create or get an account.
  count                              Create, update, or get counts. The namesake of the program.
  daily-sequence-value, dsv          Create, update, or get daily sequence values (DSVs).
  daily-sequence-value-config, dsvc  Create, update, or get daily sequence value configs (DSVCs).
  days-since                         Create and manage days since alerts.
  history                            Get the last 25 commit messages.
  l, ledger                          Create and manage ledger data.
  ip                                 Ping the service and display the ip address the server saw the request originate from.
  pdf-status                         Get the status of the pdf background services. Defaults to local time.
  ping                               Ping the service to verify configuration and availability.
  random                             Get random data.
  redirect                           Create, update, or get redirects.
  token                              Create, save, or inspect an existing token.

  TcpWtf.NumberSequence.Tool count [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

  a, add, c, create                               Create a new count.
  cat, g, get, r, read, s, show <name>            Read an existing count to see its properties.

  e, edit, set-overflow, u <name> <drops-oldest>  Set the overflow behavior for count events.

  Mark a specific invoice to be automatically reprocessed to pdf every 14d if not marked as paid.

Usage:
  TcpWtf.NumberSequence.Tool ledger invoice mark-reprocess-regularly <invoiceId> [options]

Arguments:
  <invoiceId>  The id of the invoice.

Options:
  --stamp <LocalDev|Public>        Stamp to use for the client. [default: Public]
  -v, --verbosity <Err|Info|Warn>  Verbosity level for logging. [default: Warn]
  --raw                            Show raw json object(s) instead of the nicer summary format.
  -?, -h, --help                   Show help and usage information

commands.command: l, ledger i, invoice p, payment
commands.helpInvocation: l, ledger i, invoice p, payment --help

result.stdout:
Description:
  Manage payments on an invoice.

Usage:
  TcpWtf.NumberSequence.Tool ledger invoice payment [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  a, add, c, Create <invoiceId>                  Create a payment for an existing invoice.
  e, edit, u, update <invoiceId> <paymentId>     Update the date or details of an existing payment.
  d, delete, remove, rm <invoiceId> <paymentId>  Delete a payment from an existing invoice.
  add-bulk, bulk, create-bulk                    Add payments for the full balance to multiple invoices at once with a shared payment date and details.

  -v, --verbosity <Err|Info|Warn>  Verbosity level for logging. [default: Warn]

result.stdout:
Description:
  Get random data.

Usage:
  TcpWtf.NumberSequence.Tool random [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  8ball        Get a magic 8-ball response.
  coin         Flip a coin.
  gibbs        Get a Gibbs rule.
  guid         Get a new guid.
  name         Get a random name.
  name-moby    Get a random Moby name.
  name-ubuntu  Get a random Ubuntu release name.
  no           Get a no.
  razor        Get a philosophical razor.
  wot          Get a Wheel of Time intro.
  xkcd         Get an XKCD comic reference.
  identicon    Generate an identicon image.
  bit          Get a random 1-bit number (0-1).
  crumb        Get a random 2-bit number (0-3).
  nibble       Get a random 4-bit number (0-15).
  byte         Get a random 8-bit number (0-255).
  short        Get a random 16-bit number (0-65535).
  int          Get a random 32-bit number (0-4294967295).
  long         Get a random 64-bit number (0-18446744073709551615).
